title: Sign the Meridian Pipeline?
briefing: The Meridian pipeline — Bakken crude from Williston, North Dakota, to the refineries of Port Arthur, Texas — is one signature away from breaking ground. Backers promise cheaper fuel and 28,000 construction jobs; Lakota water protectors, Sandhills ranchers, and climate groups have chained themselves to the Missouri River crossing. Energy for the grid, or a decade in federal court?

choices.label: Approve the pipeline
choices.result: Steel ships to Williston within weeks and crude futures dip $3 a barrel. Protest camps swell at the Missouri crossing and injunctions stack up in a Bismarck courtroom.

news.headline: Meridian greenlit: 28,000 jobs and energy independence flow
President paves a carbon superhighway through treaty land
Administration approves long-disputed Meridian pipeline
POTUS SIGNS THE PIPE — Dakota prairie ERUPTS! 🛢️🔥

social.text: two years of steady work just landed in my lap. about time.
they ran it under our river. we're not moving.

choices.advisor: The jobs are real and immediate. So are the lawsuits — you'll own half the Eighth Circuit's docket for a decade.

choices.label: Demand a costly reroute
choices.detail: Bend it east, around the reservation and the aquifer.
choices.result: You order the route bent 140 miles east, clear of the reservation and the Ogallala Aquifer. Both sides grumble — too expensive for industry, too compromised for purists — but the camps quietly fold.

news.headline: President forces 140-mile pipeline reroute to cool tensions
Reroute adds $4 billion and three years to vital project
A partial win: the aquifer is spared, the pipe is not
POTUS BENDS THE PIPE — nobody's happy, everybody's loud! 😤

social.text: a compromise where both sides are annoyed? that's basically governing.

choices.advisor: Splitting the baby costs money and thrills nobody, but it empties the protest camps. Sometimes quiet is the win.

choices.label: Kill the project
choices.result: You revoke the federal permit for good. Climate groups celebrate outside the White House; crude futures twitch and 28,000 promised paychecks vanish from the Dakotas overnight.

news.headline: President kills Meridian — a line in the sand for the climate
28,000 jobs torched to please activists
Administration revokes Meridian pipeline permit
POTUS PULLS THE PLUG — Williston boomtown goes BUST! 💸

social.text: my grandkids will drink from the Missouri. worth every angry headline.

choices.advisor: A legacy-defining no. Just know the laid-off welders in Williston vote too, and they'll remember.

title: Cap the Smokestacks?
briefing: Your EPA has drafted the strictest carbon caps in American history — 55 percent below 2005 levels by 2035, no exemptions. Hit the targets and you lead the world on climate; miss the politics and every shuttered plant from Pittsburgh to Gary becomes an ad about higher bills. How hard do you squeeze?

choices.label: Impose the strict cap
choices.result: The 2035 targets become law with no soft landing. Green investors cheer, steelmakers along the Ohio River warn of layoffs, and your polling in Ohio and Pennsylvania sags six points.

news.headline: Boldest climate cap in history clears the desk
War on industry: mandate menaces mills from Gary to Weirton
President signs sweeping emissions limits
POTUS SLAMS the smokestacks — bills set to SOAR?! 🌫️

social.text: first time akron's asthma map might actually shrink. i'm crying.

choices.advisor: History will love this; Youngstown won't. The economy takes a real bruise before the upside lands.

choices.label: Phase it in slowly
choices.detail: Long runway, industry credits, softer targets.
choices.result: You set gentle targets that don't bite until 2030, with credits for early movers. The reaction is a collective shrug — exactly the goal.

news.headline: President opts for gradual emissions phase-in
Industry breathes easier under softened timeline
Climate cap watered down before the ink dries
POTUS goes EASY on the smoke — greens grumble 😒

social.text: a timeline i can actually plan around. i'll take it.

choices.advisor: Pragmatic and quiet. You buy peace now and trade away a chunk of the legacy headline.

choices.label: Scrap the caps entirely
choices.result: You shelve the caps and lean into 'energy abundance.' The Dow rallies 600 points and industry sends thank-you notes; 400 climate scientists sign an open letter.

news.headline: Common sense wins: job-killing caps shelved
President abandons the climate fight before it starts
Administration drops proposed emissions caps
POTUS KILLS the carbon rules — planet left on read! 🌍❌

social.text: you can't run a country on good intentions. growth first.

choices.advisor: Wall Street loves it today. The history books are already drafting a less flattering chapter.

title: The Klamath Megafire
briefing: After a record drought, three fires in the Klamath National Forest have merged into one 800,000-acre blaze straddling the California-Oregon line. Yreka is evacuating, Medford is next, and the smoke has turned skies orange as far east as Boston. Throw everything at the flames now, or hold resources and confront why this keeps happening?

choices.label: Surge every resource now
choices.result: You federalize the response and the sky over the Klamath fills with tankers. The line holds outside Yreka and the cameras catch you hugging hotshot crews at dawn.

news.headline: President mounts massive federal firefight
POTUS GOES ALL IN on the megafire — YREKA SAVED at dawn! 🔥🚁
Heroic response, but the drought question goes unanswered
Disaster tab hits $11 billion as Washington opens the spigot

social.text: watched the tankers turn the fire back from our ridge. thank you isn't enough.

choices.advisor: Showing up wins the week. Just don't let 'we fought hard' become an excuse to skip 'why again?'

choices.label: Fund prevention & resilience
choices.detail: Controlled burns, forest thinning, building codes.
choices.result: You pair the response with a $6 billion resilience package — controlled burns across the Sierra, fire-wise codes, drought infrastructure. Forest scientists applaud; some voters wanted you on the fire line instead.

news.headline: President treats the cause, not just the smoke
Administration unveils wildfire resilience plan
More mandates and burn rules dumped on rural counties
POTUS lights MORE fires (on purpose?!) to stop the big one 🤔🔥

social.text: controlled burns work. we've begged for this funding for 20 years.

choices.advisor: Smart, unglamorous, and it pays off three fire seasons from now — long after the cameras leave.

choices.label: Leave it to the states
choices.result: You call fire a state matter and keep federal cash on a short leash. Gov. Elena Bruno of California fumes on live TV, the fire jumps Highway 96, and Happy Camp — a town you could have saved — burns on the evening news.

news.headline: President holds the line on federal spending
Happy Camp burns as Washington shrugs
President defers wildfire response to states
POTUS SITS OUT the megafire — Sacramento RAGES! 😡🔥

social.text: we called for help and got a press release. our street is gone.

choices.advisor: Fiscally tidy, politically radioactive. A disaster is the one place 'not my job' never plays.

title: Mandate the Electric Future?
briefing: Your draft rule would end sales of new gas-engine cars by 2038. Detroit is split, Michigan autoworkers are nervous, and you can still drive I-90 across Montana without passing a fast charger. Floor it toward the electric future, or pump the brakes before the public revolts?

choices.label: Set the 2038 deadline
choices.result: You lock in the 2038 phase-out. Helios Motors jumps 12 percent and battery towns from Georgia to Tennessee boom; truck country hears 'they're taking your engine' and the backlash writes itself.

news.headline: President sets the date the gas engine ends: 2038
Government picks your next car — and it isn't ready
Administration mandates EV transition timeline
POTUS BANS the gas pedal (eventually) — pickup nation REVOLTS! ⚡🚗

social.text: our gigafactory just announced a third shift. the future has a tennessee zip code now.

choices.advisor: The right long arc, the wrong charging map. You'll be defending range anxiety at every town hall west of the Mississippi.

choices.label: Incentivize, don't mandate
choices.result: You pour $30 billion into rebates and a coast-to-coast charger buildout and let buyers choose. Adoption climbs steadily and almost nobody feels forced — but the gas era doesn't end on schedule.

news.headline: President bets on carrots over mandates for EVs
Consumer choice survives the green push
Voluntary plan may be too slow to matter
POTUS BRIBES you to go electric — free charger near you?! 🔌💰

social.text: took the rebate, love the car, nobody twisted my arm. this is how you do it.

choices.advisor: Popular and durable. It's slower than the science wants, but a rule nobody resents actually sticks.

choices.label: Shelve the mandate
choices.result: You side with the engine and kill the timeline. Dealers and gearheads celebrate; Georgia battery startups feel the rug pulled while China's EV makers lap the field.

news.headline: President saves the American gas engine
EV future stalls out in the driveway
Administration drops EV phase-out plan
POTUS REVS the V8 — climate crowd FUMING! 🏎️💨

social.text: you can have my gas pedal when you pry it from my cold dead foot. WE WON.

choices.advisor: A crowd-pleaser at the pump. The transition you're skipping is happening in Shanghai with or without you.

title: Sell Yellowstone's Doorstep?
briefing: Cordillera Resources has offered $14 billion for gold and copper rights on Yellowstone's northeastern edge, above Cooke City, Montana. The money could fund the entire park system for a decade; the headwaters of the Lamar River could never be unspoiled again. Cash the check, or keep the postcard?

choices.label: Lease the buffer lands
choices.result: You open the buffer lands above Cooke City and earmark the windfall for trails and rangers. Conservationists call it a bribe to wreck the Beartooth front.

news.headline: Park system self-funds for a decade on Montana deal
President sells Yellowstone's edge to the highest bidder
Interior leases Yellowstone buffer lands for mining
POTUS PUTS YELLOWSTONE ON SALE — now with a mine! ⛏️🏞️

social.text: ranger jobs AND mining jobs in one valley? our school stays open.

choices.advisor: The math is seductive and the scenery is finite. Once the Beartooth front goes, no budget buys it back.

choices.label: Reject it and expand the park
choices.result: You not only decline — you fold the disputed 30,000 acres into Yellowstone forever. Hikers weep with joy; Cordillera and cash-strapped Park County do not.

news.headline: Historic: president grows Yellowstone instead of selling it
President expands Yellowstone, rejects mining bid
$14 billion locked away for a pretty view
POTUS SUPERSIZES YELLOWSTONE — miners LOCKED OUT forever! 🌲🚫

social.text: walked the beartooth for 30 years scared it'd be gone. now it never will be.

choices.advisor: The cleanest legacy move on the board. Park County wanted that money, and they'll make you feel it.

choices.label: Order an environmental study
choices.result: You order a multi-year impact review of the Lamar headwaters and decide nothing today. Both camps accuse you of stalling — which is, precisely, the plan.

news.headline: President orders study on Yellowstone buffer lands
Decision dodged as red tape buries a deal
Conservationists wary of a quiet path to the mine
POTUS hits SNOOZE on the park fight — see you in 2030! 😴

social.text: 'we're studying it' is the most presidential way to say 'not today.'

choices.advisor: A study buys time and offends everyone a little. Useful when you genuinely don't want to choose yet.

title: The Colorado Runs Dry
briefing: Arizona and California are at each other's throats over the shrinking Colorado River, with Lake Mead 40 feet from dead pool. Imperial Valley growers hold rights dating to 1901; Phoenix is rationing showers and Gov. Marta Reyes of Arizona calls the residence daily. There isn't enough water for everyone to win.

choices.label: Broker a forced compact
choices.result: You lock Reyes and Gov. Ellen Nakamura of California in the Roosevelt Room until they sign. Nobody gets all they wanted, but the lawsuits stop and the taps stay on in Phoenix.

news.headline: President brokers historic Colorado River compact
A fair-ish peace in the western water war
Imperial Valley forced to give up century-old water rights
POTUS SPLITS THE COLORADO — one very angry handshake! 🤝💧

social.text: the city can shower again. i don't care who's mad, it worked.

choices.advisor: Thankless and durable. A compact you force today is a war you don't fight every summer.

choices.label: Back the Imperial Valley farms
choices.result: You defend the 1901 water rights and the Imperial Valley keeps its flow. The winter lettuce harvest is saved; Phoenix and Las Vegas tighten rationing and seethe.

news.headline: President protects the farmers who feed the nation
Phoenix rations while alfalfa drinks the river dry
Administration upholds senior Colorado River rights
POTUS PICKS THE FARMS — Phoenix faucets run DRY! 🚿❌

social.text: this water built our farm since 1907. finally someone respects that.

choices.advisor: Feeds the nation and the base. The five million thirsty voters in Phoenix and Vegas are a bigger electorate.

choices.label: Prioritize the cities
choices.result: You route emergency flows to Phoenix, Tucson, and Las Vegas. Faucets recover and unrest cools — while fallowed fields and furious growers dominate the Imperial Valley press.

news.headline: President keeps five million people from running dry
Emergency Colorado River flows redirected to desert cities
Crops left to die so Vegas can keep its fountains
POTUS SAVES THE CITIES — farmers watch fields TURN TO DUST! 🌾💀

social.text: 20 days of brown-water warnings just ended. you have no idea.

choices.advisor: More people, more stability, more food-price blowback. There's no clean answer when the river loses.

title: The Last Mine Closes
briefing: Cardinal Hollow, the country's last major coal mine, is days from closing for good — taking 1,100 jobs in McDowell County, West Virginia with it. You can't save the coal — but you can decide what the families in Welch wake up to. Prop up the past, or pay to build them a future?

choices.label: Fund a green-jobs transition
choices.result: You announce a $2 billion retraining and clean-energy package built around Welch. It's hopeful and expensive, and the miners are watching to see if 'transition' is more than a brochure.

news.headline: President turns a dying coal town toward the sun
Federal package aims to retrain McDowell County miners
Washington bets miners want to build wind turbines
POTUS swaps COAL for SOLAR in West Virginia — will it work?! ☀️⛏️

social.text: skeptical but i signed up for the training. first hope this valley's had in years.

choices.advisor: The compassionate, future-facing play. If the new jobs don't materialize fast, the cynicism comes back doubled.

choices.label: Subsidize the mine to stay open
choices.result: You write nine-figure checks to keep Cardinal Hollow open a few more years. Welch cheers and Sen. Bill Hartline calls it 'a promise kept'; economists call it money on fire.

news.headline: President stands by coal country's workers
Taxpayers prop up a mine the market already buried
Administration subsidizes last operating coal mine
POTUS REVIVES the mine — coal's back, baby?! (for now) ⛏️💵

social.text: my dad mined here, his dad too. you kept our way of life alive.

choices.advisor: Buys loyalty and time, not a future. You're paying to delay the exact same conversation in three years.

choices.label: Let it close, offer relocation aid
choices.result: You let the market run its course and offer relocation checks to help families leave. It's honest and unsentimental, and McDowell County feels written off.

news.headline: Cardinal Hollow closes as president offers relocation aid
Coal's last chapter ends, but Welch is left behind
President abandons coal families to 'the market'
POTUS to West Virginia: HERE'S A CHECK, GOOD LUCK! 📦😔

social.text: they don't want us to move. they want us to disappear. message received.

choices.advisor: Economically clean, humanly brutal. 'I gave you a check and a map' is not a speech that ages well.

title: Pledge at the Geneva Summit
briefing: Every major nation is in Geneva for the UN climate summit, cameras rolling, waiting to hear your number. A bold pledge makes you the world's climate leader but binds your economy to hard targets; a cautious one keeps your hands free while China's Premier Wen Zhao smirks from the front row. The podium is yours.

choices.label: Make the most ambitious pledge
choices.result: You commit to a 65 percent cut by 2035 — the deepest in the room — and rally the island states behind you. The Geneva hall stands and claps; back home, industry reads the fine print and groans.

news.headline: President leads the world with a landmark Geneva pledge
President pledges 65 percent emissions cut in Geneva
Grand promises in Geneva, painful bills back home
POTUS WOWS GENEVA — signs away the smokestacks on LIVE TV! 🌍🎤

social.text: watched the whole hall stand up. our president just led the planet. chills.

choices.advisor: A standing ovation in Geneva. Now you owe the world a delivery you'll be judged on for years.

choices.label: Offer a credible modest pledge
choices.result: You pledge a 40 percent cut you can actually hit and dare Beijing to match it. The German and Japanese delegations nod approvingly; activists wanted fireworks and got a spreadsheet.

news.headline: President offers measured, achievable climate target
Sensible: no reckless promises in Geneva
Cautious pledge falls short of the moment
POTUS plays it SAFE on the world stage — activists yawn 😴

social.text: a promise you can keep beats a headline you can't. underrated move.

choices.advisor: Quietly the smart play — a target you hit beats a target you announce. Just don't expect a parade.

choices.label: Refuse to bind your economy
choices.result: You decline to sign anything binding and put growth first. Oil futures and sovereignty hawks cheer; Berlin and Tokyo mutter about American retreat as Premier Wen Zhao fills the vacuum by lunch.

news.headline: President refuses to mortgage the economy abroad
President walks out of Geneva as the world commits
President declines binding pledge at Geneva summit
POTUS GHOSTS GENEVA — allies SHOOK! 🌍🚪

social.text: finally a president who won't let geneva set our energy policy. respect.

choices.advisor: Protects the economy and your independence. It also hands Beijing the moral high ground on a platter.

title: Revive Nuclear Power?
briefing: Lodestar Energy says its next-generation small reactors could deliver clean, round-the-clock power from the Clinch River site in Tennessee within eight years. But 'nuclear' still spooks the public, the waste still has nowhere to go but Yucca Mountain, and the buildout runs $90 billion. Bet big on the atom, or steer clear of the word entirely?

choices.label: Launch a nuclear buildout
choices.result: You greenlight a 12-reactor fleet from Tennessee to Wyoming and the clean-firm-power crowd is ecstatic. NIMBY hearings ignite in every proposed county and the Three Mile Island signs come out of the attic.

news.headline: President bets the climate on a nuclear revival
Finally: clean power that works at 3 a.m.
Bold on carbon, but the waste question won't go away
POTUS GOES NUCLEAR — glow-in-the-dark jokes EVERYWHERE! ☢️⚡

social.text: clean baseload that doesn't care if the wind blows. this is the unlock. FINALLY.
a reactor 8 miles from my kids' school? absolutely not. see you at the hearing.

choices.advisor: Clean, firm, and politically scary. The physics is on your side; the memory of Three Mile Island is not.

choices.label: Fund a small pilot program
choices.detail: One demo reactor, prove it first.
choices.result: You bankroll a single demo reactor at Clinch River and promise to scale only if it works. Cautious and credible — boosters wanted a fleet, skeptics wanted nothing.

news.headline: President funds demo reactor at Tennessee's Clinch River
Smart first step toward clean, reliable power
A toe in the water while the climate clock ticks
POTUS builds ONE tiny reactor — baby steps to the atom! ⚛️🍼

social.text: prove it on one site before you bet the grid on it. this is how adults do nuclear.

choices.advisor: Low-risk, low-reward. A pilot buys data and cover, and quietly delays the real decision to a future you.

choices.label: Rule out new nuclear
choices.result: You take the atom off the table and double down on prairie wind, desert solar, and storage. Anti-nuclear voters relax; grid engineers warn you just removed your only clean backstop for the dark, windless nights.

news.headline: President closes the door on nuclear risk
President benches the one clean source that runs nonstop
Administration rules out new nuclear plants
POTUS says NO to nukes — grid engineers SWEAT the night shift! 🌙😬

social.text: no new reactors, no waste my grandkids inherit. thank you for listening.

choices.advisor: Popular and intuitive. It also bets the clean grid entirely on the weather cooperating — every single night.
